whpb_20210208_press_d_gpt-5-2025-08-07.readability_explanation: The summary uses a clear hierarchy with an Overview followed by well-labeled sections and consistent bullet points, making key information easy to locate. Formatting is uniform across sections with descriptive headings that create strong visual structure. A minor drawback is the dense, multi-clause Overview paragraph and some redundancy where bullets reiterate paragraph content, which slightly reduces scannability.
whpb_20210208_press_d_gpt-5-2025-08-07.fluency_explanation: Writing is grammatically sound with precise, professional wording and generally smooth sentence flow. The repeated use of full titles in every bullet (e.g., “Acting Administrator Slavitt…”) and some long, multi-clause sentences in the Overview introduce mild wordiness. Overall, the language is clear and concise enough for easy reading with only minor awkwardness.
whpb_20210208_press_d_gpt-5-2025-08-07.coherence_explanation: Ideas are logically organized: vaccination progress and equity lead into pandemic trends, variants, and dosing strategy, followed by Q&A topics like travel testing, schools, and state policies. Each section keeps related points together and avoids scattering information. Headings serve as effective transitions, producing a smooth, logical flow that mirrors a briefing and Q&A sequence.
whpb_20210208_press_d_gpt-5-2025-08-07.tone_explanation: The tone is consistently professional and objective, relying on neutral reporting verbs such as “reports,” “notes,” and “emphasizes.” It avoids subjective or emotional language and maintains appropriate formality throughout. The summary presents viewpoints even-handedly, suitable for stakeholder review.

whpb_20210208_press_d_gpt-5-2025-08-07.completeness_explanation: {"overall": {"score": 0.936, "total_covered_items": 19, "detail_distribution": {"rich": 18, "adequate": 1, "sparse": 0, "barebone": 0}, "summary": "Overall, the candidate preserves the core meaning and key operational details for almost all covered items at a rich level. It captures who said what, the rationale behind policies, quantitative context (where material), and the conditions/implications for public health actions. One minor area is Slavitt\u2019s explicit framing of mask-wearing as life-saving, which is conveyed overall but not directly tied to Slavitt; otherwise, detail is strong across vaccination priorities, variant surveillance, two-dose strategy reasoning, guidance on travel and school reopening, and federal engagement with states."}, "covered_items": {"topics": [{"topic": "Acting Administrator Slavitt discusses the impact of wearing", "points": [{"id": "t_001_p_001", "text": "Acting Administrator Slavitt discusses the impact of wearing masks in saving lives, the ongoing efforts to vaccinate those most at risk, and the focus on prioritizing the elderly, seniors, frontline healthcare workers, and essential workers.", "detail_score": 0.8, "detail_level": "adequate", "assessment": "The candidate thoroughly covers Slavitt\u2019s emphasis on prioritizing high\u2011risk groups (seniors, long\u2011term care, healthcare and essential workers) and ongoing vaccination efforts, but it does not explicitly attribute the \u201cmasks save lives\u201d point to Slavitt (mitigation is emphasized elsewhere in the briefing). Core prioritization and vaccination focus are preserved; explicit attribution of mask impact to Slavitt is missing."}]}, {"topic": "Acting Administrator Slavitt discusses the progress of vaccination", "points": [{"id": "t_002_p_001", "text": "Acting Administrator Slavitt discusses the progress of vaccination efforts, particularly in long-term care facilities and among the elderly, while acknowledging the need to prioritize vaccinations for racial and ethnic communities that have been disproportionately affected by the pandemic.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate provides concrete metrics for long-term care and seniors and explicitly highlights equity for disproportionately affected racial and ethnic communities. Progress and the equity prioritization are clearly articulated with supporting detail."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky provides an update on the declining", "points": [{"id": "t_003_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky provides an update on the declining COVID-19 cases and hospital admissions, but emphasizes the need to remain vigilant due to the presence of variants and urges people to continue wearing masks, practicing social distancing, avoiding crowded spaces, and getting vaccinated.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Declines in cases/hospitalizations are detailed along with the warning about high absolute levels and variant threats. The candidate includes specific mitigation guidance (masking, distancing, avoiding travel/crowds) and encouragement to get vaccinated."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Fauci explains that while there is a", "points": [{"id": "t_004_p_001", "text": "Dr. Fauci explains that while there is a discussion about the possibility of studying the efficacy of a single dose of the COVID-19 vaccine, it is not practical due to the time it would take and the availability of vaccines, and emphasizes that getting both doses is important for maximum protection against the virus and its variants.", "detail_score": 0.96, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly explains why a one\u2011dose efficacy study is impractical and reiterates the importance of on\u2011time second doses for maximal protection, including against variants, adding rationale about antibody titers and breadth."}]}, {"topic": "Kaitlan Collins asks Dr Fauci about the estimated", "points": [{"id": "t_005_p_001", "text": "Kaitlan Collins asks Dr. Fauci about the estimated number of COVID-19 variants and the potential benefits of requiring testing for domestic flights.", "detail_score": 0.92, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Both facets of the question are addressed: uncertainty about true variant numbers due to limited sequencing and the discussion of potential domestic flight testing as a mitigation. The candidate names the reporter and situates the issues in Q&A."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky explains that the increasing number of", "points": [{"id": "t_006_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky explains that the increasing number of reported variants is partly due to increased sequencing efforts, which have revealed more variants and are expected to uncover even more in the future.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate explicitly attributes rising variant counts to increased sequencing and notes expectations of finding more, supplemented with concrete descriptors of scale (e.g., tenfold increase)."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky explains that they are planning to", "points": [{"id": "t_007_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky explains that they are planning to increase sequencing efforts in collaboration with state labs, commercial labs, and academic labs in order to better understand the number and proportion of variants.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Plans to expand sequencing with state, commercial, and academic partners are captured along with the purpose (understanding counts and proportions), plus added details about increasing volume and sampling from every state."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky and Acting Administrator Slavitt emphasize that", "points": [{"id": "t_008_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky and Acting Administrator Slavitt emphasize that they are unable to provide an estimate on the actual higher number of variants at the moment, but advise against domestic travel due to current circumstances.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate notes they decline to estimate true variant prevalence and reiterate the advice to avoid domestic travel. Both uncertainty and the travel recommendation are preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky and Acting Administrator Slavitt emphasize the", "points": [{"id": "t_009_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky and Acting Administrator Slavitt emphasize the importance of increased screening in places where people gather, particularly airports, to detect asymptomatic cases, while also advising against domestic travel and suggesting testing as a mitigation measure if necessary.", "detail_score": 0.96, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate reflects the emphasis on increased screening at airports to find asymptomatic cases, the primary advice to avoid travel, and testing as an additional mitigation when travel occurs."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Fauci states that there is currently no", "points": [{"id": "t_010_p_001", "text": "Dr. Fauci states that there is currently no evidence to suggest that the mutant strains of the virus are becoming less virulent, and it is uncertain if the efficacy of current vaccines will be dampened or lessen as a result.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate states there is no evidence of reduced virulence and flags uncertainty regarding vaccine efficacy against variants, adding context about ongoing evaluation. Core uncertainty and current evidence are preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Fauci states that it is currently uncertain", "points": [{"id": "t_011_p_001", "text": "Dr. Fauci states that it is currently uncertain if the mutant strains of the virus will become less virulent or if the efficacy of current vaccines will be affected, and further observation and studies over the next couple of years will provide more information.", "detail_score": 0.93, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Uncertainty about virulence and efficacy is captured, and the candidate explicitly notes that two\u2011year trial follow\u2011up and ongoing studies will inform answers over time."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Fauci emphasizes that currently the vaccines are", "points": [{"id": "t_012_p_001", "text": "Dr. Fauci emphasizes that currently, the vaccines are highly effective against the dominant wild-type virus in the country, with an efficacy rate of 94 to 95 percent.", "detail_score": 1.0, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate directly states 94\u201395% efficacy against the dominant wild\u2011type strain; key figures and context are intact."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Fauci expresses concern that the 117 variant", "points": [{"id": "t_013_p_001", "text": "Dr. Fauci expresses concern that the 117 variant could become dominant by the end of March, but reassures that the current vaccines are effective against it and emphasizes the importance of vaccinating as many people as possible and implementing public health measures.", "detail_score": 0.98, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate mentions the end\u2011of\u2011March dominance projection, confirms vaccine effectiveness against B.1.1.7, and underscores rapid vaccination and public health measures."}]}, {"topic": "Acting Administrator Slavitt addresses questions about the total", "points": [{"id": "t_014_p_001", "text": "Acting Administrator Slavitt addresses questions about the total number of doses set aside for the long-term care program and the potential integration of separate registration systems for the new pharmacy program, emphasizing the need for more supply and assistance in finding vaccination locations.", "detail_score": 0.93, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate details LTC dose metrics, notes incomplete breakdowns, explains that pharmacies will initially use their own systems, and acknowledges the need to help people find sites, aligning with the emphasis on supply and access assistance."}]}, {"topic": "Kristen Welker asks for the Acting Administrator's assessment", "points": [{"id": "t_015_p_001", "text": "Kristen Welker asks for the Acting Administrator's assessment of states that have relaxed their COVID-19 protocols, specifically mentioning Iowa.", "detail_score": 0.9, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate recounts the question context (states rolling back restrictions, such as Iowa) and provides the ensuing discouragement of relaxation. The essence of the inquiry is preserved."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky discourages states and local jurisdictions from", "points": [{"id": "t_016_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky discourages states and local jurisdictions from relaxing COVID-19 protocols, emphasizing the need for continued mitigation measures due to the ongoing high number of cases and the threat of emerging variants.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Firm discouragement of relaxing measures is captured with reasons: high case counts and variant threats, and the need to continue mitigation."}]}, {"topic": "Dr Walensky emphasizes that the majority of COVID", "points": [{"id": "t_017_p_001", "text": "Dr. Walensky emphasizes that the majority of COVID-19 infections in schools come from the community, and to ensure safe school reopenings, it is crucial to decrease community spread through continued adherence to mitigation measures such as social distancing and masking.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The candidate clearly states that most school infections originate from the community and links safe reopening to reducing community spread and maintaining mitigation in schools."}]}, {"topic": "Isaac Stanley Becker asks about the administration's efforts", "points": [{"id": "t_018_p_001", "text": "Isaac Stanley-Becker asks about the administration's efforts to communicate CDC guidance and discourage governors from relaxing mitigation measures.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "The question about federal communication with governors is reflected, and the candidate outlines the administration\u2019s approach to discourage easing restrictions."}]}, {"topic": "The administration is actively communicating with states including", "points": [{"id": "t_019_p_001", "text": "The administration is actively communicating with states, including through weekly governors calls and one-on-one conversations, to encourage them to decrease community spread and follow sensible public health measures in order to combat the virus and its variants.", "detail_score": 0.95, "detail_level": "rich", "assessment": "Active communication via weekly calls and one\u2011on\u2011one conversations is detailed, along with the intent to encourage reduction of spread and adherence to public health measures."}]}], "decisions": []}}

whpb_20210208_press_d_gpt-5-2025-08-07.candidate_claims: {"claims": [{"text": "Key participants included Andy Slavitt (Senior White House COVID-19 advisor), Dr. Rochelle Walensky (CDC Director), and Dr. Anthony Fauci (NIAID Director).", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_001"}, {"text": "The Long-Term Care Program has administered over 4.8 million doses to more than 3.7 million people.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_002"}, {"text": "In skilled nursing facilities with at least one vaccination clinic, a median of almost 80 percent of residents have received at least one dose.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_003"}, {"text": "Of more than 40 million vaccine doses administered nationally, over 17 million have gone to people aged 65 or older.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_004"}, {"text": "The administration is using the Defense Production Act to increase vaccine supply.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_005"}, {"text": "The government is creating more vaccination locations, including large community sites and retail pharmacies.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_006"}, {"text": "More vaccinators are being mobilized by authorizing retired clinicians and deploying the military.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_007"}, {"text": "Further expansion of vaccination efforts is linked to Congress delivering the American Rescue Plan to the President\u2019s desk.", "claim_type": "risk_or_issue", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_008"}, {"text": "Average daily cases between January 31 and February 6 are 119,900, nearly 20 percent lower than the prior week.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_009"}, {"text": "New COVID-19 hospital admissions average 9,977 per day, down about 17 percent from the previous week.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_010"}, {"text": "More than 83,000 Americans are currently hospitalized with COVID-19.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_011"}, {"text": "Average reported deaths were 3,221 per day, up 2.4 percent from the prior period.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_012"}, {"text": "A delayed report of 1,570 deaths from one state spanning several months contributed to the recent increase in reported deaths.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_013"}, {"text": "As of February 7, there were 699 confirmed variant cases across 34 states, including 690 B.1.1.7 cases.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_014"}, {"text": "The Pfizer and Moderna vaccines achieve about 94 to 95 percent efficacy with the full two-dose regimen used in phase 3 trials.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_015"}, {"text": "A large randomized study comparing one versus two doses would take several months.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_016"}, {"text": "A single mRNA vaccine dose produces neutralizing antibodies above a protective threshold.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_017"}, {"text": "The second mRNA vaccine dose increases neutralizing antibody titers roughly tenfold.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_018"}, {"text": "Higher antibody titers from a second dose broaden protection to cover both wild-type virus and key variants such as B.1.1.7 and B.1.351.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_019"}, {"text": "Widespread suboptimal immunity from only one dose could theoretically exert selective pressure favoring the emergence of additional mutant strains.", "claim_type": "risk_or_issue", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_020"}, {"text": "The administration continues to follow two-dose regimens for Pfizer and Moderna.", "claim_type": "decision", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_021"}, {"text": "Modeling projects that the B.1.1.7 lineage could become dominant in the United States by the end of March.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_022"}, {"text": "Current mRNA vaccines are quite effective against the B.1.1.7 variant.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_023"}, {"text": "Vaccine efficacy is lower against the B.1.351 variant first identified in South Africa.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_024"}, {"text": "There is no current data indicating that SARS\u2011CoV\u20112 is mutating into a less virulent strain.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_025"}, {"text": "Initial UK reports of increased virulence of B.1.1.7 require further confirmation and remain under evaluation.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_026"}, {"text": "Phase 3 vaccine trials are designed to run for two years with ongoing follow-up and FDA reporting, so efficacy data will continue to accrue.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_027"}, {"text": "Current vaccines remain highly efficacious\u201494 to 95 percent\u2014against the dominant wild-type virus circulating in the United States.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_028"}, {"text": "Reported variant case numbers are increasing in part because sequencing volume has risen roughly tenfold in the past three weeks.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_029"}, {"text": "CDC is expanding collaborations with state public health labs, commercial labs, and academic centers to enhance genomic surveillance.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_030"}, {"text": "Sequencing volume is anticipated to increase to three to four times the current enhanced level in the coming weeks.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_031"}, {"text": "The administration does not yet have a precise breakdown of first versus second doses or the percentage of fully vaccinated long-term care residents.", "claim_type": "risk_or_issue", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_032"}, {"text": "Many retail pharmacies participating in vaccination will rely on their existing registration systems in the near term.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_033"}, {"text": "New CDC guidance on school reopening will be released in the coming days.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_034"}, {"text": "Most infections in schools are introduced from the community.", "claim_type": "observation", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_035"}, {"text": "In-school transmission is low when masking and distancing are consistently applied.", "claim_type": "observation", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_036"}, {"text": "Dr. Walensky joins at least one weekly governors call.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_037"}, {"text": "Dr. Walensky has spoken individually with numerous governors about mitigation policies.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_038"}]}

whpb_20210208_press_d_anthropic.claude-s.readability_explanation: The summary demonstrates excellent visual hierarchy with clear main sections and consistent subsection formatting. The bullet points are well-organized and uniformly formatted throughout. However, the extremely long opening paragraph in the Overview section creates a dense wall of text that is difficult to scan, preventing a perfect score.
whpb_20210208_press_d_anthropic.claude-s.fluency_explanation: The writing is highly polished with sophisticated sentence structures and precise language throughout. Technical terms are used appropriately and sentences flow naturally. Minor issues include occasional complex sentences that could be simplified for easier reading, but overall the prose is professional and clear.
whpb_20210208_press_d_anthropic.claude-s.coherence_explanation: The summary follows excellent logical organization, grouping related topics together and presenting information in a natural flow that mirrors the meeting progression. Each section builds logically on previous content, and transitions between topics are smooth. The structure effectively moves from high-level updates to specific policy discussions and Q&A responses.
whpb_20210208_press_d_anthropic.claude-s.tone_explanation: The tone is consistently professional, objective, and appropriate for stakeholder review throughout. The language remains neutral and factual, avoiding subjective judgments or casual expressions. The formal register is maintained consistently across all sections, making it suitable for executive-level distribution.

whpb_20210208_press_d_anthropic.claude-s.candidate_claims: {"claims": [{"text": "Key participants included Andy Slavitt (Senior White House COVID-19 advisor), Dr. Rochelle Walensky (CDC Director), and Dr. Anthony Fauci (NIAID Director).", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_001"}, {"text": "The Long-Term Care Program has administered over 4.8 million doses to more than 3.7 million people.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_002"}, {"text": "In skilled nursing facilities with at least one vaccination clinic, a median of almost 80 percent of residents have received at least one dose.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_003"}, {"text": "Of more than 40 million vaccine doses administered nationally, over 17 million have gone to people aged 65 or older.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_004"}, {"text": "The administration is using the Defense Production Act to increase vaccine supply.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_005"}, {"text": "The government is creating more vaccination locations, including large community sites and retail pharmacies.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_006"}, {"text": "More vaccinators are being mobilized by authorizing retired clinicians and deploying the military.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_007"}, {"text": "Further expansion of vaccination efforts is linked to Congress delivering the American Rescue Plan to the President\u2019s desk.", "claim_type": "risk_or_issue", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_008"}, {"text": "Average daily cases between January 31 and February 6 are 119,900, nearly 20 percent lower than the prior week.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_009"}, {"text": "New COVID-19 hospital admissions average 9,977 per day, down about 17 percent from the previous week.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_010"}, {"text": "More than 83,000 Americans are currently hospitalized with COVID-19.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_011"}, {"text": "Average reported deaths were 3,221 per day, up 2.4 percent from the prior period.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_012"}, {"text": "A delayed report of 1,570 deaths from one state spanning several months contributed to the recent increase in reported deaths.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_013"}, {"text": "As of February 7, there were 699 confirmed variant cases across 34 states, including 690 B.1.1.7 cases.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_014"}, {"text": "The Pfizer and Moderna vaccines achieve about 94 to 95 percent efficacy with the full two-dose regimen used in phase 3 trials.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_015"}, {"text": "A large randomized study comparing one versus two doses would take several months.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_016"}, {"text": "A single mRNA vaccine dose produces neutralizing antibodies above a protective threshold.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_017"}, {"text": "The second mRNA vaccine dose increases neutralizing antibody titers roughly tenfold.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_018"}, {"text": "Higher antibody titers from a second dose broaden protection to cover both wild-type virus and key variants such as B.1.1.7 and B.1.351.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_019"}, {"text": "Widespread suboptimal immunity from only one dose could theoretically exert selective pressure favoring the emergence of additional mutant strains.", "claim_type": "risk_or_issue", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_020"}, {"text": "The administration continues to follow two-dose regimens for Pfizer and Moderna.", "claim_type": "decision", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_021"}, {"text": "Modeling projects that the B.1.1.7 lineage could become dominant in the United States by the end of March.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_022"}, {"text": "Current mRNA vaccines are quite effective against the B.1.1.7 variant.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_023"}, {"text": "Vaccine efficacy is lower against the B.1.351 variant first identified in South Africa.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_024"}, {"text": "There is no current data indicating that SARS\u2011CoV\u20112 is mutating into a less virulent strain.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_025"}, {"text": "Initial UK reports of increased virulence of B.1.1.7 require further confirmation and remain under evaluation.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_026"}, {"text": "Phase 3 vaccine trials are designed to run for two years with ongoing follow-up and FDA reporting, so efficacy data will continue to accrue.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_027"}, {"text": "Current vaccines remain highly efficacious\u201494 to 95 percent\u2014against the dominant wild-type virus circulating in the United States.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_028"}, {"text": "Reported variant case numbers are increasing in part because sequencing volume has risen roughly tenfold in the past three weeks.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_029"}, {"text": "CDC is expanding collaborations with state public health labs, commercial labs, and academic centers to enhance genomic surveillance.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_030"}, {"text": "Sequencing volume is anticipated to increase to three to four times the current enhanced level in the coming weeks.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "conditional", "claim_id": "c_031"}, {"text": "The administration does not yet have a precise breakdown of first versus second doses or the percentage of fully vaccinated long-term care residents.", "claim_type": "risk_or_issue", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_032"}, {"text": "Many retail pharmacies participating in vaccination will rely on their existing registration systems in the near term.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_033"}, {"text": "New CDC guidance on school reopening will be released in the coming days.", "claim_type": "key_update", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_034"}, {"text": "Most infections in schools are introduced from the community.", "claim_type": "observation", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_035"}, {"text": "In-school transmission is low when masking and distancing are consistently applied.", "claim_type": "observation", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_036"}, {"text": "Dr. Walensky joins at least one weekly governors call.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_037"}, {"text": "Dr. Walensky has spoken individually with numerous governors about mitigation policies.", "claim_type": "context", "assertion_strength": "explicit", "claim_id": "c_038"}]}
